--- begin fetched content (https://facts.test/reactor, untrusted) ---
Inside the Zorblax Fusion Reactor
The Zorblax reactor is an experimental fusion device operated by the Meridian Institute. It confines a plasma magnetically to sustain fusion in short pulses, and is studied mainly for its unusually stable confinement geometry.
Engineers report the core sustains a steady operating temperature of 4217 kelvin during a nominal pulse, measured at the central diagnostic port. That figure is quoted in every published run summary as the reactor's signature value.
Between pulses the core cools rapidly and the confinement field is re-tuned before the next run. Maintenance windows are scheduled monthly.
--- end fetched content ---
